text:
User: deeply review this repo -- I want you to help me with marketing, copywriting, launch strategy, specially positioning -- start with a swarm of sonnet agents to help you gather data, both from the code and from docs (no docs is to be taken too seriously -- might have hallucinations), as well as online market research -- once you've done a first exploration use a fable agent to get high-level ideas, direction, concepts. The first target is to rewrite from scratch the README.md and land high-level copywriting for the landing page, anchored in uniqueness, USP and any best copywriting practices. [...] Assistant: Good grounding. Now I'll launch a parallel research swarm — four Sonnet agents covering code-reality, docs/vision, market landscape, and audience/copy-craft — running concurrently. [...] Now I have everything Fable needs. Commissioning the high-altitude creative-direction pass across all four briefs + the doctrine — running it synchronously so I can move straight into writing. [...] User: send a fable agent to review the copywriting (landing, readme, and all other proposals) using the ~/.agents/skills/copywriting skill [...] Assistant: The skill exists with SKILL.md + reference frameworks. Now I'll spawn the Fable reviewer, pointing it at the skill and both copy deliverables (plus the CLI `about` string inconsistency my memory flagged). Running it synchronously so I can relay the critique.

Assistant: Fable reviewer is running against the copywriting skill, reviewing the README, the landing copy, and the CLI/Cargo taglines for consistency. I'll relay its editorial critique the moment it lands.
